1 If they say its 0 interest its definitely 0 unless you breach the contract and miss payments It is also 0 on the entirety of the purchase including VAT 2 There usually is a processing fee by the bank and Tabby Usually like a fixed fee of AED49 or 13 of the total amount Check before proceeding compare 3 The entire amount is blocked and released monthly as you pay it off correct 4 Theres usually a 150VAT to close an installment plan with the repayment of the remaining amount No other penalty or interest 5 Done this many times over at Apple and other places Worth it if you dont want to pay the full amount in one go And yes the numbers match every time Remember this is technically a loan facility and the bank should send you a breakdown of your loan with fixed monthly installments and fees clearly stated Its pretty standard issue straight forward Just manage your finances and pay on time and youre good to go for what ever the repayment period is Good luck

I think it has something to do with Sweden not experiencing a big war or political upheaval for centuries Even if you suppress income equality to minimum if there is a continuous accumulation of wealth through generations there will high wealth inequality Also Sweden has relatively low inheritance tax which maximizes generational accumulation Korea Japan which have high inheritance tax being relatively low on wealth inequality support that theory

While there is definitely truth to the inheritance tax explanation these sort of comparisons usually ignore pensions which heavily distorts the picture the bigger the welfare state is Your regular Swede probably relies mainly on their pension accounts not on personal wealth whereas people in countries with a weaker welfare system invest heavily in housing and other assets that are included in the statistics Sweden is also popular for being the first European country that introduced something similar to an IRA401k based on stocks but with a mandatory countrymanaged option for everyone which is basically a lowcost index fund It has been highly successful but if those accounts are not represented as wealth it definitely drags down the median
Income is heavily taxed Wealth isnt taxed at all neither is inheritance Hence income inequality is very low while wealth inequality is very high

Yes this is one the things that doesnt get talked about given that Sweden is viewed as an egalitarian country However one thing I think that softens the optics is the concept of lagom A Swedish billionaire is likely going to have a Warren Buffett type of life when it comes to being modest about their wealth In contrast an American billionaire is going to do everything in their power to flaunt their wealth to the press Jeff Bezoss wedding for example would be considered an insane violation of lagom
This claims that median wealth is higher in New Zealand than Switzerland I have my doubts

Not shocking when you understand that Sweden is very much a class society Salarymen are equal but there is class of old money industrial families that sort of keep the economy going Its great in the sense that Sweden is less dependent of foreing capital compared to peer countries but old money is old money They do erode democracy
And honestly outside of internet forums I very rarely see people complain about it

Strikes as more consistent with wealth inequality being a stupid metric than with Sweden having a wealth inequality problem For example if Sweden had a weaker social safety net the middle class would be incentivized to save more and accumulate more wealth Also the fact that Sweden is perhaps the only European country to have reasonably successful public and private venture capital equity markets is surely relevant here given that your chart indicates financial assets are uniquely important for Swedens wealth inequality Would Sweden be better off it did was unable to produce valuable tech companies Finally the fact that Sweden has not been bombed to bits and its most successful companies were never subject to nationalization are both unambiguously good things that contribute to Swedens wealth inequality

75 of the variation in median wealth across developed countries is basically 1 Is the median personhousehold a homeowner and 2 If so how much equity do they have in their home Which is basically a function of how much home prices have risen and the age of the median householdhomeowner

In New Zealand the homeownership rate is 66 in Switzerland 42 My theory is that the median New Zealander owns a home with several years of value increase after purchasing That can easily be tens of thousands of dollars Meanwhile the median Swiss person is a renter with all their wealth in savings and investment

As a Spaniatd I also am very much skeptical median wealth in my country is higher than it is in the US Ireland Japan Finland Sweden barely any lower than it is in the Netherlands Norway France Canada but then again according to this we are also after India the second country where on average gross financial assets represent the lowest share gross nonfinancial assets represent the highest share of gross wealth so that may be why this is in our case

Yeah if Im being honest I deliberately chose to frame the post in a little bit of a somewhat clickbaity manner the truth is that I had already noticed these data when I first read this UBS Global Wealth Report 2025 a couple months ago XD so I couldnt possibly have been shocked this morning when I made this post even when I fist noticed this a couple months back it didnt truly shock me either but it did very much catch my interest my attention my curiosity

Also I follow Scandinavian politics very very closely am fairly knowledgeable about it as well Id say it was through the Scandinavian party systems that I first realized the extent to which class still plays a huge role in Scandinavian society to this day One of the most fundamental characteristics about the party systems of all three of these countries is how profoundly installed it is in the collective imagery the psyche of Danes Swedes Norwegians the notion that all the political parties that happen to belong to their respective party systems can be sorted into either one or the other of two fundamentally distinct ideologically atodds blocs that divide the political spectrum completely in two in all three of these countries A socialistredredgreen bloc encompassing the Democratic
